I treated the prior turns as a proposal log, not as proof. The prompt fixes the syntax: sigma,tau are distinguished tame generators, the relation tau^sigma = tau^2 is implicit, later generators must lie in the pro-2 core, and exponents are supplied modulo M=85667662080.The prior log‚Äôs only unrefuted candidate is the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9‚Äď40; the earlier scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 candidates were explicitly rejected there by finite-tame or index-two tests.

The candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Turn 40‚Äôs final displayed formulas are still inverted. The conclusions mostly survive, but any final proof must use the following directions.For the sign-cover calculation, let V=kerőļ, let t be the deck involution, and work on a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block where ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the Fox coefficient is the geometric seriesDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.

The correct identity isDa=1‚ąítőĪa 1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít,not its reciprocal. The reason is simply that (tőĪa)ŌÄ=t, because ŌÄ‚Č°1(mod2) and ŌÄ‚Č°0(mode) on an e-th tame-character block. Since 1‚ąít lies in the radical of the 2-adic sign-cover group algebra and 1‚ąítőĪa is a unit for őĪaÓÄ†=1, Da is radical. Therefore the coefficient contributed byx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. Thus the intended conclusion survives: nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots to the sign-kernel abelianization. But the usable formula isDa=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít.The trivial tame-inertia block has the same issue. In the convention where T is the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

If f=2a m with m odd, then s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). For even f, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. The valid elimination is therefore

Y=P(T)X+YňČ,P(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,up to the harmless convention T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. It is not legitimate to use the reciprocal in A=F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1).With h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T‚ąí1),one gets, for any nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Hence h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and the trivial block remains nonsingular. The usable formula is

P(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís.With these corrected formulas, the prior abelianization consequences still hold. For every legal finite tame quotient (e,f),Ue,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and for the sign kernel,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4.

The C2 and C4 torsion is entirely in the tame-inertia-invariant block; the nontrivial odd tame-character blocks are unit-pivot blocks.I also reframe the mixed (e,f)=(3,2) target from Turns 38‚Äď40. In that quotient, writeőĒ3,2‚ČÉS3=‚ü®ŌĄ,ŌÉ‚ą£ŌĄ3=ŌÉ2=1,¬†ŌÉŌĄŌÉ‚ąí1=ŌĄ2‚ü©,and setAi=XŌĄi,Bi=XŌĄiŌÉ,i‚ąąZ/3.The candidate-side computation should be interpreted as a relation-preserving tangent class, not merely a b-component. WithZd=i‚ąąZ/3‚ąĎ[Ai,Bi+d],the candidate‚Äôs first mixed even-Frobenius class is represented byőī0 (b)=Z0,őī0(Ai)=[Bi,S],őī0(Bi)=[Ai,S],őī0 (S)=0.This representative preserves the quadratic DemuŇ°kin initial relationq=i‚ąĎ(Ai[2]+Bi[2])+[b,S],because Jacobi gives cancellation of the [Z0,S] term in characteristic 2.

But the label Z0 is gauge-dependent. Replacing the tame Frobenius lift by ŌÉ‚Ä≤=ŌĄk ŌÉ cyclesZd‚Ü¶Zd+k.So the gauge-invariant statement is not ‚Äúarithmetic must give Z0 ‚ÄĚ until a specific arithmetic Frobenius lift is fixed. Without that choice, the target is the orbit {őī0,őī1,őī2}, or equivalently the assertion that the class has the same nonzero mixed augmentation-zero components as one of those three representatives.The candidate is still not proved. The hard missing step remains a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem identifying the full filtered (ŌÉ,ŌĄ)-operator action selected by the relation with the arithmetic action over all finite tame subextensions. The corrected finite-tame abelianization, cup-product, sign-kernel, and cyclotomic tests are strong evidence, but they do not by themselves determine the filtered even-Frobenius action.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I corrected the two recurring reciprocal errors. The correct formulas areDa=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít,P(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís (up to T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1). With these, the conclusionsUe,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4 still hold. I also clarified that the mixed (3,2) class is only Z0 after fixing a Frobenius-lift gauge; otherwise it is defined up to the cycle Zd‚Ü¶Zd+k.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The remaining missing ingredient is the dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, showing that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action realizes the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action for every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: Fix a standard S3 -extension, for instance L=Q2(ő∂3,32), and a Frobenius lift convention. Compute the arithmetic action on GL (2)/GL(2)3 or GL (2)/GL(2)5, with the precise target that it matches one of the relation-preserving classes őīd, and d=0 for the chosen lift.[BREAKTHROUGH]
